commands.review_summary:
The PR splits gateway service Doctor detection from repair and routes gateway service cleanup/config repair through structured health checks and the shared repair runner.

PR surface: Source +1310, Tests +570. Total +1880 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an internal Doctor repair migration PR rather than a bug report. Current-main inspection shows gateway services still use the legacy direct repair calls, while the PR head adds the structured gateway service checks and runner routing.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainer review should decide stack order and whether the WSL service-manager proof is enough for this protected-label PR.

target.title: Doctor: structure gateway service repairs
target.body:
## Summary

This PR moves the gateway services doctor pass onto the structured repair runner while keeping the legacy lint/read-only presentation intact.

- Splits gateway service detection from repair so doctor --fix can report dry-run changes, diffs, and service effects through the shared repair runner.
- Keeps the public health contract unchanged; these are core doctor internals only.
- Preserves gateway service audit behavior, including planned managed env keys, expected service PATH, install-plan warnings, token drift warnings, source-checkout warnings, and running-service rewrite blocking.
- Keeps platform notes positional and note-based so user-visible lint behavior remains the same.

## Verification

- git diff --check
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot: 4 files / 74 tests passed after rebase onto #84326
- codex review --base fork/doctor-sandbox-structured-repairs: no actionable regressions
- previous full slice proof on this branch: pnpm install, oxfmt/oxlint touched files, core and core-test tsgo, focused Vitest 4 files / 74 tests, Codex review clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `doctor --fix` can now route gateway service cleanup/config repair through structured dry-run/diff/effects while lint/read-only gateway service notes remain positional.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout CLI, disposable HOME with isolated OpenClaw state dir and a disposable user systemd unit file.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a disposable `openclaw-gateway.service` under the temp HOME with a stale embedded `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN`, missing PATH, missing network-online unit metadata, and an old `ExecStart`; then ran `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/gateway-services/config --json` and `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes --no-workspace-suggestions` through the PR source checkout. The source checkout used a temporary ignored `dist/entry.js` placeholder for the service audit prerequisite and removed it after proof.
Evidence after fix: Gateway service lint returned `ok:false` with structured `core/doctor/gateway-services/config` findings for running-service rewrite blocking, embedded stale service token, token drift, missing PATH, missing `After=network-online.target`, missing `Wants=network-online.target`, and mismatched `RestartSec`. The fix run printed `Gateway service is running; leaving supervisor metadata unchanged.` and completed. The disposable unit file remained unchanged, preserving the safety rule for running services.
Observed result after fix: Gateway service config drift is exposed as structured lint findings, the positional doctor repair path routes through the structured runner, and running-service command/entrypoint rewrites remain blocked instead of mutating a live service file.
What was not tested: Full repo check in this rerun; installing, stopping, removing, or rewriting a real launchd/systemd service on the host. Focused tests cover gateway service structured detection, planned install warnings, expected service PATH audit input, positional gateway repair runner routing, dry-run/diff option forwarding, and legacy platform-note preservation.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
